response.justification: Strong domain fit with deep geospatial and navigation expertise through GISCollective and prior GIS work, plus distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby. However, primary C++ experience is dated (~2015), and recent 7+ year career trajectory focuses on JavaScript/TypeScript application development rather than systems-level infrastructure. Would require significant ramp-up on modern C++ for a role prioritizing it as the main technical requirement, though his track record of learning new technologies and strong foundation suggest capability.
strengths: Exact domain expertise: Founded and maintains GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and prior work at Green Map Association; navigation/geospatial is core strength
Distributed systems understanding: Worked on micro-services infrastructure handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby; infrastructure experience across DevOps, Docker, CI/CD
Senior-level capabilities: Proven ability to lead complex projects, mentor others, learn new tech stacks, and operate across full technical stack
concerns: C++ skills outdated: Last modern C++ work ~2014-2015 (10 years ago); recent roles focus on JavaScript/TypeScript, risking skill fade on primary requirement
Career drift away from systems work: Last 7 years in application development (Node/TS) rather than infrastructure/pipeline systems; GIS work is now side project, not primary
No Rust alternative: Resume lists D but no Rust; cannot leverage acceptable alternative if C++ ramp-up is challenging

key_factors.factor: C++ currency and modern systems experience
